cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
cwes.description: The software constructs all or part of an SQL command using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special characters that could modify the intended SQL command when it is sent to a downstream component.
cwes.mitigation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements, validate and sanitize all user input, use stored procedures, and least privilege database accounts.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
cwes.description: The software does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controllable input before it is placed in output that is used as a web page that is served to other users.
cwes.mitigation: Use context-appropriate output encoding, implement Content Security Policy (CSP), and validate/sanitize all user input.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
cwes.description: The software constructs all or part of an OS command using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special characters that could modify the intended OS command when it is sent to a downstream component.
cwes.mitigation: Avoid using user input in OS commands, use safe APIs instead of shell execution, validate and sanitize all input, and use parameterized commands where possible.

cwes.name: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
cwes.description: The software uses external input to construct a pathname that is intended to identify a file or directory that is located underneath a restricted parent directory, but the software does not properly neutralize special characters within the pathname, causing it to resolve to a location outside of the restricted directory.
cwes.mitigation: Use a whitelist of allowed files/paths, sanitize input to remove directory traversal sequences, resolve paths and verify they are within the expected directory, and avoid using user input directly in path construction.

cwes.name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
cwes.description: The software contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key, which it uses for its own inbound authentication, outbound communication to external components, or encryption of internal data.
cwes.mitigation: Use secure credential management systems, environment variables, or configuration files outside of source code. Never commit secrets to version control.

cwes.name: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information
cwes.description: The application stores sensitive information in cleartext in a database, on the filesystem, or in another storage location.
cwes.mitigation: Encrypt sensitive data at rest using strong encryption algorithms, use secure key management, and apply least privilege access controls.

cwes.name: Improper Authentication
cwes.description: When an actor claims to have a given identity, the software does not prove or insufficiently proves that the claim is correct.
cwes.mitigation: Implement strong authentication mechanisms, use multi-factor authentication where possible, and properly validate credentials.

cwes.name: Missing Authorization
cwes.description: The software does not perform an authorization check when an actor attempts to access a resource or perform an action.
cwes.mitigation: Implement proper access control checks for all sensitive resources and actions, use role-based access control (RBAC), and validate permissions on every request.

cwes.name: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
cwes.description: The product exposes sensitive information to an actor that is not explicitly authorized to have access to that information.
cwes.mitigation: Classify data by sensitivity, implement proper access controls, avoid exposing sensitive information in error messages, logs, or responses.

cwes.name: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
cwes.description: The application deserializes untrusted data without sufficiently verifying that the resulting data will be valid.
cwes.mitigation: Avoid deserializing untrusted data, use safe serialization formats, validate and sanitize all data before deserialization, and implement integrity checks.

cwes.name: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
cwes.description: The web application does not, or cannot, sufficiently verify whether a well-formed, valid, consistent request was intentionally provided by the user who submitted the request.
cwes.mitigation: Implement CSRF tokens for state-changing operations, use SameSite cookies, validate referrer headers, and use custom request headers.

cwes.name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
cwes.description: The web server receives a URL or similar request from an upstream component and retrieves the contents of that URL, but it does not sufficiently validate the request to ensure that it is accessing only intended resources.
cwes.mitigation: Validate and sanitize all URL inputs, use allowlists for allowed domains, disable URL redirects, and use a proxy server for external requests.

cwes.name: Unprotected Storage of Credentials
cwes.description: The application stores credentials in an insecure manner.
cwes.mitigation: Encrypt credentials at rest, use secure credential stores, and limit access to credential files.

cwes.name: Hardcoded Password
cwes.description: The software contains a hardcoded password.
cwes.mitigation: Remove hardcoded passwords, use environment variables or secure vault services.

cwes.name: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference ('XXE')
cwes.description: The software processes XML documents that contain XML external entity references, allowing attackers to read files, execute commands, or cause denial of service.
cwes.mitigation: Disable external entity processing in XML parsers, use secure XML parsing configurations, and validate all XML inputs.

cwes.name: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
cwes.description: The application allows the upload of files without validating their type, potentially allowing attackers to upload malicious files.
cwes.mitigation: Implement strict file type validation, store uploaded files outside the web root, use random filenames, and scan files for malware.

cwes.name: Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input ('Classic Buffer Overflow')
cwes.description: The software copies an input buffer to an output buffer without verifying that the size of the input buffer is less than the size of the output buffer, leading to a buffer overflow.
cwes.mitigation: Use safe string handling functions, implement bounds checking, use automatic memory management, and enable compiler protections.

cwes.name: Use of Externally-Controlled Format String
cwes.description: The software uses a function that accepts a format string as an argument, but the format string is obtained from an external source.
cwes.mitigation: Do not allow user input in format strings, use fixed format strings, and validate all format string inputs.

cwes.name: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
cwes.description: The software does not properly control the allocation and release of resources, allowing attackers to consume resources excessively.
cwes.mitigation: Implement resource limits, use rate limiting, and properly manage resource allocation and cleanup.

cwes.name: Improper Input Validation
cwes.description: The software receives input or data, but it does not validate or incorrectly validates that the input has the properties required to process the data safely and correctly.
cwes.mitigation: Implement strict input validation using allowlists, validate all inputs before processing, and use type-safe data structures.

cwes.name: Incorrect Default Permissions
cwes.description: The product sets incorrect permissions on resources, which allows unintended actors to access or modify them.
cwes.mitigation: Set appropriate permissions on all resources, use least privilege principle, and review permission settings regularly.

cwes.name: Insufficiently Protected Credentials
cwes.description: The product stores or transmits credentials in a manner that provides insufficient protection.
cwes.mitigation: Encrypt credentials in transit and at rest, use secure protocols, and implement proper key management.

cwes.name: Improper Privilege Management
cwes.description: The software does not properly assign, modify, track, or check privileges for an actor, creating an opportunity for unintended access or privilege escalation.
cwes.mitigation: Implement proper privilege management, use least privilege principle, and regularly review and audit privileges.

cwes.name: Insufficient Verification of Data Authenticity
cwes.description: The software does not sufficiently verify the authenticity of data, allowing attackers to modify data without detection.
cwes.mitigation: Use cryptographic hashing, digital signatures, and integrity checks to verify data authenticity.

cwes.name: Improper Access Control
cwes.description: The software does not restrict or incorrectly restricts access to a resource from an unauthorized actor.
cwes.mitigation: Implement proper access control mechanisms, use role-based access control (RBAC), and validate permissions on every request.

cwes.name: Weak Password Requirements
cwes.description: The product does not require strong passwords, allowing attackers to guess passwords easily.
cwes.mitigation: Enforce strong password policies, require minimum password length and complexity, and implement password expiration.

cwes.name: Permissions, Privileges, and Access Controls
cwes.description: The product does not properly enforce restrictions on what authenticated users are allowed to do.
cwes.mitigation: Implement proper access controls, use principle of least privilege, and regularly audit permissions.

cwes.name: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')
cwes.description: The web application redirects a user to a URL that is specified by an untrusted source.
cwes.mitigation: Validate and sanitize redirect URLs, use allowlists for allowed domains, and avoid redirecting to user-controlled URLs.

cwes.name: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')
cwes.description: The software constructs code from user-controlled input without properly validating or sanitizing it.
cwes.mitigation: Avoid generating code from user input, use template engines with proper escaping, and validate all inputs.

cwes.name: Integer Overflow or Wraparound
cwes.description: The software performs a calculation that can produce an integer overflow or wraparound, when the logic assumes that the resulting value will always be larger than the original value.
cwes.mitigation: Use safe integer operations, implement bounds checking, and use data types with sufficient range.

cwes.name: NULL Pointer Dereference
cwes.description: The software dereferences a pointer that it expects to be valid, but the pointer is NULL.
cwes.mitigation: Check for NULL pointers before dereferencing, use defensive programming techniques, and implement proper error handling.

cwes.name: Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization ('Race Condition')
cwes.description: The software contains a race condition in a shared resource that can be exploited by an attacker.
cwes.mitigation: Use proper synchronization mechanisms, implement thread-safe code, and avoid shared mutable state.

cwes.name: Improper Link Resolution Before File Access ('Link Following')
cwes.description: The software follows a symbolic link or shortcut to a file or directory that the user did not intend to access.
cwes.mitigation: Resolve symbolic links before accessing files, validate file paths, and use secure file operations.

cwes.name: Untrusted Search Path
cwes.description: The software searches for critical resources using an externally-supplied search path that can point to untrusted locations.
cwes.mitigation: Use absolute paths for critical resources, validate search paths, and avoid using user-controlled paths.
